installApp.title: Install the GitHub App
installApp.body: To start reviewing Terraform PRs, install the Driftguard GitHub App on your organization or repository.
installApp.hint: Takes 30 seconds. No credit card required.

landing.ctaSubtitle: Install in 30 seconds. No credit card required. Works with GitHub, AWS, OpenTofu, and any Terraform-compatible toolchain.

hero.line2: We make sure they ship safer.

architecture.tooltipApp: DriftGuard is a GitHub App — no sidecar, no agent, no cloud access beyond read-only.
architecture.sectionSubtitle: DriftGuard is a GitHub App — no sidecar, no agent, no cloud access beyond read-only STS. The review pipeline runs on our infrastructure. Your Terraform never moves.
architecture.step01Body: Any Terraform or OpenTofu PR — written by an engineer or AI agent — triggers DriftGuard. We read terraform plan, infracost output, and security scans.
architecture.step02Body: The change embedding is matched against past failures across your team. If a similar incident exists, the PR comment cites it — with similarity score and link to the original incident.
architecture.step03Body: OPA‑compatible policies enforce allowed actions per environment, resource class, and risk score. Block merges, warn reviewers, or auto‑approve safe changes.
architecture.step04Body: Single high‑signal PR comment: cost delta, drift, security findings, compliance evidence (DORA / NIS2 / ISO 27001) — and citations to past failures.
architecture.step05Body: Merge result and post‑deploy state become new memory. Your agents and engineers get sharper every day.

codeIntegration.title: Install in 30 seconds.
codeIntegration.subtitle: GitHub App + repo config. No SDK, no rewrites, no infrastructure changes. Optional CLI for local pre-flight.

driftPreview.sectionTitle: Every AI agent output, reviewed before merge.
driftPreview.sectionSubtitle: Four analysis engines run in parallel — cost delta (Infracost), security (Checkov), live drift detection (STS), and semantic memory recall (pgvector). All in under 2s.
driftPreview.sectionAriaLabel: Every AI agent output, reviewed before merge.
driftPreview.subtitle: Four analysis engines run in parallel — cost delta (Infracost), security (Checkov), live drift (STS), and compliance mapping. Results appear in the PR within 2 seconds.
driftPreview.footerPrAnalysis: Review posted in 18s · risk 82/100 · 2 controls failed
driftPreview.footerDriftState: State fetched via STS AssumeRole · scan interval 5 min
driftPreview.footerFinOps: Infracost + live billing API · updated hourly

featureGrid.sectionTitle: Six layers. One review. Zero config overhead.
featureGrid.sectionSubtitle: Cost intelligence, live drift detection, security scanning, compliance evidence, semantic memory, and AI-native analysis — all triggered by a PR, all without changing your Terraform workflow.
featureGrid.hoverHint: Hover each card to see a real example

cost.title: Cost delta on every PR
cost.body: Infracost-powered monthly cost diff per resource. Threshold-based blocks. No surprises in the AWS bill at the end of the month.
cost.example:
aws_rds_cluster.prod
db.r5.large → db.r5.4xlarge
+€480/mo · threshold exceeded → BLOCKED

drift.title: Live state vs. plan diff
drift.body: Compares the PR's terraform plan against the real cloud state. Catches manual changes, orphan resources, and out-of-band edits before merge.
drift.example:
aws_security_group.web-sg
Ingress 0.0.0.0/0 added via Console
→ DRIFT DETECTED · 3h ago

security.title: Curated security findings
security.body: Checkov + AI triage. 255 rules mapped to compliance controls. Public S3 buckets, wildcard IAM, missing encryption — flagged with fix suggestions.
security.example:
aws_s3_bucket.tf-state
Public access block removed
CKV_AWS_19 · NIS2 Art.21 → BLOCKED

memory.title: Semantic memory of failures
memory.body: Every blocked deploy and compliance violation embedded and indexed. Open a similar PR — the original incident appears in the comment.
memory.example:
rds.delete.prod recalled
cos 0.94 · 2026-04-22
→ Same misconfig blocked twice

compliance.title: DORA / NIS2 / ISO 27001
compliance.body: Each finding mapped to compliance controls. Audit evidence collected on every PR — no extra workflow, no questionnaires.
compliance.example:
DORA Art.11 · NIS2 Art.21
ISO 27001 A.8.8 · CIS v8
→ Evidence exported automatically

aiNative.title: Built for agent contributors
aiNative.body: AI agents (Cursor, Devin, Claude Code) write half your IaC. DriftGuard treats them like junior engineers: reviewed, mentored, fast-tracked when safe.
aiNative.example:
agent.cursor opened PR #847
23 resources · risk 84/100
→ 2 critical findings · BLOCKED

incidents.sectionSubtitle: DriftGuard intercepts, analyses, and gates in under 2 seconds. Before the merge button is available.
incidents.subtitle: DriftGuard intercepts, analyses, and gates in under 2 seconds. Before the merge button is even available.
incidents.step01Body: GitHub sends PR events to DriftGuard the instant an agent pushes. No polling.
incidents.step02Body: Cost (Infracost), security (Checkov), drift (STS), and compliance run concurrently — not sequentially.
incidents.step03Body: pgvector cosine search matches the diff against every prior incident. If this pattern broke prod before, it shows up.
incidents.step04Body: A GitHub Check Run blocks the merge button. The agent cannot self-approve. Human review required above risk threshold 70.

metrics.connectPrompt: Connect your GitHub installation to see live workspace metrics.
metrics.liveSubtitle: Real-time aggregated data from your connected repositories.
metrics.staticSubtitle: Measurable guarantees on every Terraform PR — coverage, accuracy, and latency.

pricing.sectionSubtitle: Start free. Add repos as you grow. Cancel anytime.
pricing.footer: All plans include SOC 2 Type II (Q4 2026) · GDPR-native · EU data residency
pricing.ariaDesc: Start free. Add repos as you grow. Cancel anytime.

team.desc: Production PR reviews for human and agent contributors.

semanticMemory.sectionTitle: Your agents learn from production. Permanently.
semanticMemory.sectionSubtitle: Every incident — blocked deploy, drift event, policy violation — becomes a 384-d embedding. The next similar PR surfaces the original incident automatically.
semanticMemory.ariaLabel: Your agents learn from production. Permanently.
semanticMemory.subtitle: Every incident — blocked deploy, drift event, policy violation — becomes a 384-d vector. When an AI agent submits a similar pattern weeks later, DriftGuard surfaces the original incident with a similarity score before the merge button is available.

incidents.title: Incidents
incidents.subtitle: {open} open · {investigating} investigating · {resolved} resolved
incidents.noBody: Incidents are created automatically when DriftGuard detects high or critical events.
incidents.noActiveDesc: DriftGuard automatically creates incidents when repeated drift patterns or critical findings are detected across pull requests.

policies.title: Policy rules
policies.subtitle: {active} active · {total} total
policies.noBody: Run the seed script to create default policy templates.
policies.noPoliciesDesc: Policies enforce rules on every PR review — blocking merges, warning reviewers, or auto-approving safe changes based on severity, resource patterns, and rule IDs.
policies.createViaApiDesc: Policies are created via REST API. Use your installation's secret key.
policies.presetBlockCriticalDesc: Block merge when any critical-severity finding is detected.
policies.presetWarnExposureDesc: Warn when public exposure patterns are detected.
policies.presetBlockIamDesc: Block IAM policies with wildcard actions.
policies.exampleBlockCriticalDesc: Blocks merge when any critical-severity finding is detected.
policies.exampleWarnExposureDesc: Warns when resources matching public exposure patterns are changed.
policies.exampleRequireEncryptionDesc: Blocks unencrypted S3 buckets or RDS instances.
policies.exampleAlertIamDesc: Alerts team when IAM policies with wildcard actions are detected.

memory.title: Operational memory
memory.subtitle: {total} indexed incidents · used for similarity recall on every PR
memory.noBody: Memory is built as DriftGuard reviews PRs and records incident embeddings.

security.title: Security at DriftGuard
security.subtitle: We review other teams' infrastructure. Holding that trust requires us to maintain a high security bar ourselves.
security.disclosureBody1: If you discover a security vulnerability in DriftGuard, please report it to security@driftguard.io. We aim to respond within 24 hours and resolve critical issues within 72 hours.
security.disclosureBody2: Please do not open public GitHub issues for security vulnerabilities. We will acknowledge your report, keep you updated on our progress, and credit you in our security advisory if desired.

meta.title: Security — DriftGuard
meta.description: DriftGuard security practices, responsible disclosure, and vulnerability reporting.

compliance.title: Compliance
compliance.subtitle: How DriftGuard maps to DORA, NIS2, ISO 27001, and CIS Benchmarks.
compliance.evidencePackDesc: Need a pre-packaged compliance evidence export for your auditor? Available for SOC 2, ISO 27001, DORA, and NIS2.

dora.desc: DriftGuard provides an audit-ready trail of every infrastructure change reviewed, blocked, or approved. Findings and policy decisions are timestamped, signed, and exportable as DORA evidence packs.

nis2.desc: Every DriftGuard PR analysis flags findings against NIS2 risk categories. Incident evidence — severity, blast radius, remediation time — is structured for NIS2 reporting.

iso27001.desc: Checkov findings are mapped to ISO 27001 Annex A controls. Security assessment evidence per PR satisfies A.8.29 requirements without additional tooling.

gdpr.desc: DriftGuard processes only repository metadata and infrastructure plan output — no user data, no PII. Data residency is EU-only. DPA available on request.

soc2.desc: SOC 2 Type II audit scheduled Q4 2026 with Vanta-assisted readiness. CC8 (change management) evidence is already produced as a side effect of every DriftGuard PR review.

meta.title: Compliance — DriftGuard
meta.description: DORA, NIS2, ISO 27001, and SOC 2 compliance controls built into every PR review.

faq.q1: Is DriftGuard free for open-source projects?
faq.a1: Yes. The OSS plan is free forever, supports 1 repo, and includes up to 50 PR reviews per month with 30-day memory retention.
faq.q2: Can I try the Team plan before paying?
faq.a2: Yes. The Team plan includes a free trial — no credit card required. You can upgrade, downgrade, or cancel at any time.
faq.a3: You pay per repository connected to DriftGuard. Repos you disconnect are not billed. You can add or remove repos at any time and billing adjusts automatically.
faq.q4: What data stays in the EU?
faq.a4: All processing and storage happens in EU data centers. No Terraform plan data, secrets, or logs leave the EU region.
faq.a5: DriftGuard embeds every drift event, policy violation, and cost alert as a 384-dimension vector. When a similar change is detected, past incidents are surfaced automatically to prevent repeat mistakes.

about.title: We build the safety layer for AI infrastructure agents
about.body: DriftGuard was founded in Lisbon to solve a problem we faced ourselves: AI agents making autonomous Terraform changes with no guardrails. We built the runtime safety layer we wished existed.
about.missionTitle: Make AI-driven infrastructure safe by default
about.missionBody: Every Terraform pull request should be reviewed for security misconfigurations, cost drift, and policy violations before it reaches production. We make that happen automatically, for every team.
about.value0Body: Every feature starts from a security assumption. We treat your infrastructure as sensitive as source code.
about.value1Body: Our scanner rules, policy engine, and CLI are open-source. We believe security tools should be auditable.
about.value2Body: PR reviews should be fast and actionable. No false positives, no noise — just the signal that matters.
about.value3Body: We process only metadata. No infrastructure state is stored beyond what is needed for the review.
about.ctaBody: DriftGuard integrates with your GitHub workflow in under 30 seconds.
about.metaDesc: Learn about the team and mission behind DriftGuard, the AI runtime safety layer for Terraform agents.

cookie.notice: We use analytics cookies to understand how you use DriftGuard and improve the experience.
